title: Add discipline rules and learnings-read instruction to claude-runner.md
description:
Implement spec §6 + §2 consumption point 1 (docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md) in skills/ticks/references/claude-runner.md. (Blocked by the retro tick because it edits the same file.)

Add a 'Discipline rules' section near the existing 'Run continuously' guidance:
- Scope never shrinks: the agent may split, merge, or reorder ticks, and scope may grow (bugs, discovered gaps) — but only the human removes scope. If the epic-close retro's outside-in verification finds an undelivered scope item, fix it now; never relabel it 'follow-up'.
- No known-failure closes: a tick cannot close with failing acceptance. No 'close with known issues' — it passes, or it stays open/blocked/awaiting.
- Name the stall instinct: completing a large body of work triggers the instinct to summarize and hand control back; epic boundaries with a close-out tick are waypoints, not stopping points.
- Two-tier stopping rule: intra-roadmap epic boundaries auto-continue (unless gated with --awaiting checkpoint / --requires approval); the roadmap end or a gate is the hard stop where a completion report is written.

Also update the implementer prompt template's Instructions list: add as step 1 'Read .tick/learnings.md (if present) — accumulated gotchas from earlier epics' (renumber following steps). Do NOT mention .tick/config.md here — a separate tick introduces that convention and wires it in.

Files: skills/ticks/references/claude-runner.md only.
acceptance_criteria: Discipline rules section present with all 4 rules; implementer prompt template instructs reading .tick/learnings.md from the worktree; existing template content otherwise unchanged
